description: Settlement of a complicated dispute in Fustat recording that Abu l-Ḥasan b. Numayla had rented from Abu al-Ma'ali Shemuel b. Yehuda a house in Alexandria for two years (rent: 9 dinars). Abu Sa'd, the former tax farmer of Benha Sa'adya b. Efrayim b. Hudhayfa, had given 8 dinars, which he had owed to Ben Numayla, to Abu l-Ma'alī. The latter retained the 8 dinars as payment for the rent of Ibn Numayla, who had meanwhile died. But as Abu Sa'd had to return the debt to Ibn Numayla’s heirs, he had to take recourse to Abu Ma'ali; ca. 1165., in the hand of Shemuel b. Seadya ha-Levi. (Information from Goitein's index cards). VMR and EMS
